agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 complementarity while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions. Mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric fit in SSTR2-specific TM6 pocket, increasing selectivity margin.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disru

report.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting conserved-pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. However, the low pass rate suggests the need for stricter structural/docking filters to ensure only high-quality candidates are considered.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, focus on mutating non-pharmacophore positions that are likely to form conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are critical for off-target binding.

These mutations are designed to break hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions with conserved residues in SSTR3/SSTR5 pockets, reducing off-target binding without affecting SSTR2 pharmacophore interactions.
